definition: Cross-currency basis widening serves as an early warning for dollar funding stress. A -40bp threshold signals elevated stress. Leading property varies by crisis type: endogenous crises show 2-4 weeks advance signal (GFC basis widening began July 2007, ~14 months before Lehman); exogenous shocks like COVID show no lead time. The mechanism is sound but the leading indicator property is weaker than originally claimed.
evidence: GFC: basis widening July 2007, ~14 months before Lehman collapse
European sovereign crisis: ~3 months before acute Greek stress
March 2020: concurrent with shock onset, no lead time
Banks reduce FX swap counterparty exposure at first sign of uncertainty
2-4 week lead time documented across multiple crisis episodes
Ranked at top of stress indicator hierarchy by lead time and signal quality
Only 10-20bp additional widening now needed vs ~40bp pre-GFC buffer
open_questions: What distinguishes basis widening that leads to crisis from widening that reverses?
Has the post-GFC regulatory environment changed the signal's reliability?
Whether reduced buffer means earlier false signals or genuinely reduced systemic resilience
